access.anon: Can query the public academic catalog and bus timetable through /api/graphql.
access.user: Can use the public queries and the authenticated read-only Viewer fields allowed by their Session or OAuth feature scopes.
access.admin: Uses the same read-only Viewer surface as a regular user; no GraphQL governance fields are exposed.
access.agent: Can inspect the GraphQL SDL and the empty persisted-operation manifest through authenticated MCP resources; arbitrary GraphQL execution is not exposed as an MCP tool.

rules.read-only: The endpoint exposes public catalog/bus queries and authenticated Viewer reads; mutations remain deferred.
rules.single-endpoint: GraphQL HTTP requests use /api/graphql with GET, POST, and OPTIONS handlers.
rules.production-discovery: GraphiQL and schema introspection are disabled in production.
rules.request-budgets: Requests are limited to 64 KiB, one operation per HTTP request, five seconds, depth 8, 10 top-level fields, 15 aliases, 10 directives, 1000 tokens, and cost 5000; operation cost weights every paginated selection by its effective pageSize, including values supplied through variables.
rules.pagination: Every collection, including Viewer todos, subscribed sections, homeworks, schedules, and exams, uses PageInput; inputs default to page 1 and pageSize 20, and pageSize must be between 1 and 100.
rules.resolver-boundary: Resolvers call feature-owned services only; they do not issue internal HTTP requests or access Prisma directly.
rules.request-cache: The principal and entity DataLoaders are request-scoped; authentication is resolved once per request and every response is marked no-store.
rules.safe-errors: Expected authentication, authorization, and bad-input GraphQLErrors retain safe codes and status metadata; unexpected resolver failures use Yoga error masking and never expose internal details.
rules.viewer-scopes: Query.viewer returns null for an anonymous caller. Its profile, overview, todos, subscribedSections, homeworks, schedules, and exams fields independently require me:read, dashboard:read, todo:read, subscription:read, homework:read, schedule:read, and exam:read for OAuth principals; trusted-origin Sessions use their existing user authority.
rules.viewer-ownership: Viewer collections derive ownership from the authenticated user relation in feature services and never accept sectionIds or internal visibility/shape flags from GraphQL inputs.
rules.zoned-datetime: DateTime inputs accept only valid ISO 8601 datetimes with an explicit Z or numeric timezone offset, with identical coercion for variables and inline literals; schedule and exam DateTime filters normalize to their Asia/Shanghai calendar day before querying @db.Date columns.
rules.schema-evolution: The canonical SDL snapshot is checked for drift and breaking changes in CI.

fields: Public semester, course, section, teacher, and bus queries
Read-only; no arbitrary GraphQL execution through MCP

queries.name: viewer
notes: Returns null for anonymous requests; each selected Viewer child field enforces its exact feature read scope.

resources.name: graphql_operations_manifest
resources.description: Persisted-operation manifest; intentionally empty until controlled persisted operations are introduced.
